ending: Tala returns to Bandfast as a Fused Archon, but she is still settling and chooses to postpone refining for one to two years. Holly discovers that the consumption scripts created excessive physical impurities, then revises Tala's scripts until the burden begins to decline. The eventual refining process will be especially painful. Tala remains estranged from her parents while cautiously rebuilding ties with all fourteen siblings. She schedules repeated Caravan Guild journeys to Marliweather, continues paying her debt, and leaves Nalac to decide whether he still wants magic. Her eldest sister begins a conditional six-month iron-paint contract with the Constructionist Guild. Alat remains Tala's collaborator and gains remote Archive reading access, though it still cannot write publicly or send messages. Terry remains unbonded, develops a knife-based combat method, and begins mounted-combat training with Tala. Rain continues his own difficult advancement work and plans to travel with Tala through winter. The blood-eyed arcane's identity, motives, location, and continuing threat remain unknown.
in_short: Tala develops iron body paint that accelerates her bodily enhancement, recovers memories proving that a blood-eyed arcane attacked her mind, and turns ending-berry seeds into a controlled anti-magic breath. After surviving a battle in which a griffin escapes and her mage-hunter companion kills another magical beast, she travels to Marliweather to confront the family who sent her away under their debt. Tala rejects renewed ties with her parents but apologizes to her fourteen siblings, begins rebuilding those relationships, and helps her eldest sister secure a trial contract for the paint. Emotional resolution with her siblings lets Tala and Alat complete fusion together. Back in Bandfast, Tala learns that her scripts have created impurities that make early refining unsafe, so she adopts a one-to-two-year timetable. She plans regular family visits and caravan work while the hostile arcane remains unidentified and at large.

recaps.text: Previously: Tala entered this volume as an Archon, a Caravan Guild mage, and a protector whose unconventional inscriptions continually reshaped her capabilities. Terry was already her intelligent creature companion, and Kit served as her bonded dimensional storage. Lynn was an established friend and fellow Caravan Guild mage, Holly handled inscriptions, and Ingrid worked as an archivist. Tala's existing magical abilities, professional ties, and close companions formed the foundation for her next stage.

So far this book: Tala reports concerns about suppressed Culinary Guild research, then nearly destroys Kit's doorway and badly injures herself while testing a gravity-driven tungsten projectile. Her regeneration consumes enormous reserves. Lynn helps her establish Kit's doorway in their shared home and apply iron body paint that reflects escaped magical power back through Tala, accelerating her enhancements at the cost of a much greater appetite. Because the paint conceals Tala's magical signature, Bandfast's defenses restrain her until Jenna intervenes. A through spike then supplies a recognizable signature and the illusion of ordinary skin. Alat reconstructs memories that Tala had lost. They show a blood-eyed arcane manipulating her attention and belief, attacking her mind, and destroying recent memories before retreating. Tala gives Jenna the recovered evidence for Bandfast's authorities, but the attacker's identity and purpose remain unknown. Lynn also agrees to take Kannis, Tala's former academy acquaintance, as her mageling and housemate. Tala accepts the arrangement, while she and Alat establish safer notice and consent rules for changes to Tala's perception.

So far this book: Tala's iron paint strengthens the pressure behind her inscriptions but hides her magical signature, so Jenna arranges a through spike that presents both an identifying signature and a normal appearance. Alat recovers memories proving that a blood-eyed arcane interfered with Tala's mind, and Bandfast's leadership receives the evidence. Lynn formally takes Kannis as a Caravan Guild mageling, and Holly improves Kannis's inscription plan. Training reveals Terry's exceptional teleportation and combat power, while the guard captain gives Tala and Terry a new direction in mounted and weapon coordination. Tala then uses an ending-berry harvesting trip as bait for the unknown arcane, accompanied by a mage hunter who controls iron dust. No attacker appears. Instead, their magical activity draws a griffin and an amorphous magical beast. Tala and Terry repeatedly cripple the rapidly healing griffin with a gravity-bound tungsten sphere, but it wounds Tala and escapes with the sphere still lodged near its spine. The mage hunter destroys the second beast, completing the hardest combat requirement for his advancement. Tala survives with heavy injuries and depleted defenses, and the hunter parts from her on good terms.

So far this book: After recovering from the northern battle, Tala reports to Holly and receives the next stage of her bodily modification scripts. She and Alat propose using an activated ending-berry seed as a breath weapon. Under Holly's supervision and with a healer present, Tala proves that her internal protections can contain the seed's dissolution effect and direct it outward. Further tests let her shape the destructive exhalation without injuring herself. Alat also takes a larger role in managing Tala's movement toward fusion, shortening the expected timetable. Tala then leaves Bandfast with a caravan bound for Marliweather. During the journey, the caravan overseer explains how she guides magic through spaces within iron, while Rain discusses his slower fusion progress and supports Tala through her anxiety about the coming family encounter. Tests against the overseer's defenses show that Tala's ending-seed breath destabilizes ordinary barriers, though a compartmentalized barrier that continuously rebuilds itself can resist the attack. The caravan reaches Marliweather safely. Tala arrives still unsure whether she hates her parents, whether she wants reconciliation, or how she will face a family whose debt decision shaped her childhood.

So far this book: Tala returns to Marliweather but initially avoids her family. In a childhood park she meets Nalac, the younger brother who barely remembers her, protects him from older boys, and follows him home. The first confrontation overwhelms her. Her father offers the family's savings toward her debt, but Tala makes clear that being sent to the academy without a direct conversation destroyed their relationship. She refuses renewed contact with her parents, apologizes to her siblings for abandoning them, and asks to rebuild those relationships separately. All fourteen siblings meet with her. They forgive her conditionally, making clear that trust will require sustained effort. Nalac admits that finding Tala helped motivate his interest in magic, leaving him uncertain whether he still wants that path. Tala does not decide for him. She also offers her eldest sister an iron-paint business opportunity with the local Constructionist Guild. As Tala reaches a calmer view of her past and chooses a future with her siblings, she and Alat recognize their gate and soul as shared and complete fusion together. Master Hoffest warns the newly Fused Tala to settle before refining and agrees to meet the family alchemist about the paint.

Tala completes the book as a Fused Archon. In Marliweather she keeps her parents at a distance but continues repairing her relationships with all fourteen siblings, including a safe magical play session with Terry and the younger children. Her eldest sister purchases the iron-paint formula and secures a six-month Constructionist Guild trial for ten gallons each month. Tala then builds regular Caravan Guild travel between Bandfast and Marliweather into her winter plans, giving the family reconciliation a practical way to continue while she keeps paying her debt. Rain improves his magical capacity and density through difficult meditation and joins Tala's winter travel, though his intended advancement remains incomplete. Alat gains remote read access to the Archive but still cannot send messages or write outside the private shared space. Terry remains unbonded, develops a combat method using repeated movement between thrown knives, and begins mounted coordination with Tala. Holly discovers that Tala's consumption scripts produced unusually high physical impurities. After revisions, the impurity burden begins to decline, but it will complicate refining. Tala therefore rejects an early attempt and chooses a one-to-two-year period for settling. The blood-eyed arcane remains unidentified, its motives and location unresolved.
